description: The Garduña is a mythical secret criminal society said to have been founded in Spain in the late Middle Ages, around 1417, with statutes approved in Toledo in 1420. It was allegedly involved in robbery, kidnapping, arson, and murder-for-hire. However, historians consider it a fictional organization, with all references traced back to a 19th-century book. It is often cited as a foundational myth for Italian mafias like the Camorra, Mafia, and 'Ndrangheta.
